name: Project Scaffolding & AppleScript Engine
theme: Build the foundation — project structure, CLI skeleton, and the core AppleScript execution layer that everything else depends on.

criteria.description: pyproject.toml exists with project metadata (name='mailctl', python>=3.11), dependencies on typer and rich, and a [project.scripts] entry point mapping 'mailctl' to the app
testSteps: Read pyproject.toml
Verify [project] has name = 'mailctl' and requires-python >= '3.11'
Verify dependencies include 'typer' and 'rich'
Verify [project.scripts] has mailctl entry point
criteria.threshold: All four checks pass

criteria.description: Project is installable via pip install -e . in a virtualenv and the 'mailctl' command becomes available on PATH
testSteps: Create and activate a virtualenv
Run pip install -e .
Run 'mailctl --help' and verify it produces output with exit code 0
criteria.threshold: mailctl --help exits 0 and prints usage info

criteria.description: Source code is organized under src/mailctl/ package with an __init__.py and a clear module structure
testSteps: Verify src/mailctl/__init__.py exists
Verify src/mailctl/cli.py (or main.py) exists with the Typer app
Verify src/mailctl/applescript.py (or engine.py) exists for the AppleScript engine
criteria.threshold: Package structure is clean with separate modules for CLI and engine

criteria.description: .gitignore includes Python-standard entries (*.pyc, __pycache__, .venv, *.egg-info, dist, build)
testSteps: Read .gitignore
Verify it contains entries for __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv, *.egg-info, dist, build
criteria.threshold: All standard Python entries present

criteria.description: mailctl --version prints a version string and exits 0
testSteps: Run 'mailctl --version'
Verify output contains a version number (e.g., '0.1.0')
Verify exit code is 0
criteria.threshold: Version output and exit code 0

criteria.description: mailctl --help prints a well-structured help message showing available commands/groups
testSteps: Run 'mailctl --help'
Verify output includes the program description
Verify output lists available commands or command groups
Verify exit code is 0
criteria.threshold: Help text is present, structured, and informative

criteria.description: Typer app is created with a callback that handles --version and --json global options. The app structure supports subcommand groups (accounts, mailboxes, messages, etc.) even if they are placeholder-only in this sprint
testSteps: Read the CLI module
Verify a Typer app is instantiated
Verify --version is handled via a callback
Verify the app structure supports adding subcommand groups
criteria.threshold: Typer app with callback and extensible group structure

criteria.description: All osascript subprocess calls go through a single function (single execution seam) so tests can mock one point
testSteps: Read the AppleScript engine module
Verify there is exactly one function that calls subprocess.run with osascript
Verify all other engine functions delegate to this single function
Grep the codebase for subprocess.run — it should only appear in the engine module
criteria.threshold: Single osascript execution seam confirmed

criteria.description: The engine function accepts an AppleScript string, executes it via 'osascript -e', and returns the stdout as a string on success
testSteps: Read the engine's execute function
Verify it calls subprocess.run with ['osascript', '-e', script]
Verify it captures stdout and returns it
Verify it uses text mode or decodes bytes
criteria.threshold: Function signature and implementation match specification

criteria.description: The engine supports multi-statement AppleScript execution (batching multiple operations in a single osascript call) to minimize process startup overhead
testSteps: Read the engine module
Verify the execute function can accept multi-line AppleScript strings
Check unit tests demonstrate multi-statement scripts executing correctly
criteria.threshold: Multi-statement scripts are supported and tested

criteria.description: The engine parses AppleScript return values: at minimum strings and comma-delimited lists into Python types (str, list)
testSteps: Run unit tests for the parser
Verify string return values are parsed to Python str
Verify list/record return values are parsed to Python list/dict
criteria.threshold: Parser handles strings and lists correctly

criteria.description: Engine raises a distinct AppleScriptError (or similar custom exception) when osascript fails, with the stderr message preserved
testSteps: Verify a custom exception class exists for AppleScript errors
Verify the engine raises it when osascript returns non-zero exit code
Verify the stderr output is available in the exception
criteria.threshold: Custom exception with stderr message on failure

criteria.description: Engine detects and categorizes 'Mail.app not running' errors into a specific error type or message that downstream code can handle
testSteps: Run unit test that simulates osascript failing because Mail.app is not running
Verify the error is categorized as a 'not running' type (via exception subclass, error code, or message pattern)
criteria.threshold: Mail-not-running is distinguishable from generic errors

criteria.description: Engine detects and categorizes 'automation permission denied' errors into a specific error type that downstream code can handle
testSteps: Run unit test that simulates osascript failing due to permission denial
Verify the error is categorized as a 'permission denied' type
criteria.threshold: Permission-denied is distinguishable from generic errors

criteria.description: Engine detects and categorizes generic AppleScript execution errors (syntax errors, runtime errors) with the original error message preserved
testSteps: Run unit test that simulates an AppleScript syntax/runtime error
Verify the error includes the original AppleScript error message
Verify it is distinguishable from permission-denied and not-running errors
criteria.threshold: Script errors are categorized and preserve original message

criteria.description: Engine enforces a configurable timeout on osascript calls (default reasonable, e.g. 30s) and raises a timeout-specific error when exceeded
testSteps: Read the engine execute function
Verify subprocess.run is called with a timeout parameter
Verify a timeout results in a specific error type or message
Check unit test for timeout handling
criteria.threshold: Timeout is enforced and produces a clear error

criteria.description: CLI entry point catches AppleScript errors and prints user-friendly messages to stderr with appropriate exit codes (non-zero)
testSteps: Verify there is error handling at the CLI level that catches engine exceptions
Verify errors are printed to stderr (not stdout)
Verify exit code is non-zero on error
criteria.threshold: Errors go to stderr with non-zero exit codes

criteria.description: pytest is configured in pyproject.toml with testpaths and markers for integration tests
testSteps: Read pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] section
Verify testpaths includes tests/
Verify markers include 'integration'
criteria.threshold: pytest config present with integration marker

criteria.description: A reusable mock fixture exists that intercepts osascript subprocess calls and allows tests to set canned responses or errors
testSteps: Read conftest.py or a fixtures module
Verify a pytest fixture exists that patches subprocess.run (or the engine's execute function)
Verify the fixture allows configuring return values and simulating errors
criteria.threshold: Mock fixture exists and is reusable

criteria.description: Unit tests exist for the AppleScript engine covering: successful execution, multi-statement scripts, error parsing, timeout, and all error categories
testSteps: Run pytest for the engine test module
Verify tests cover: success case, multi-statement, mail-not-running error, permission-denied error, script error, timeout
Verify all tests pass
criteria.threshold: All engine unit tests pass with >= 6 distinct test cases

criteria.description: Unit tests exist for the CLI skeleton covering: --version output, --help output, and error display
testSteps: Run pytest for CLI tests
Verify tests cover --version, --help, and error rendering
Verify all tests pass
criteria.threshold: All CLI skeleton tests pass with >= 3 distinct test cases

criteria.description: Full test suite runs successfully with 'pytest' from the project root and all tests pass
testSteps: Run 'pytest -v' from project root
Verify all tests pass (0 failures)
Verify no warnings about missing fixtures or imports
criteria.threshold: All tests pass, exit code 0

criteria.description: Test directory structure is organized: tests/ at the project root, with tests/unit/ for unit tests and tests/integration/ for integration tests (even if integration is empty this sprint)
testSteps: Verify tests/ directory exists
Verify tests/unit/ or tests/ contains unit test files
Verify tests/integration/ directory exists (may be empty or have a placeholder)
criteria.threshold: Test directories present and organized

criteria.description: stderr is used for error messages and stdout is used for data output — this pattern is established in the CLI error handling
testSteps: Read CLI error handling code
Verify errors are written to stderr (e.g., via typer.echo to stderr, or rich.console with stderr=True)
Verify normal output goes to stdout
criteria.threshold: Clear stdout/stderr separation in code

criteria.description: Exit codes follow the spec convention: 0 for success, 1 for general error, 2 for usage error
testSteps: Verify exit code constants or patterns in the codebase
Verify CLI raises SystemExit or uses typer.Exit with appropriate codes
Check tests verify exit codes
criteria.threshold: Exit code convention implemented and tested

outOfScope: Rich table output formatting (Sprint 2 — only the foundation/console setup in this sprint)
